target.scope: entire cart microservice (init + DB layer + handler)

incident.title: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion under sustained AddItem load
incident.description: Under sustained high-concurrency AddItem traffic, p99 latency rises sharply and a small fraction of requests start failing with context-deadline-exceeded. Without observability, on-call cannot tell whether the bottleneck is (a) PG connection pool saturation, (b) slow individual queries, (c) lock contention in Redis cache write-through, or (d) gRPC server goroutine saturation.
operator_questions: Is the PG connection pool currently exhausted?
What is the p50/p99 of individual SQL queries under load?
What fraction of AddItem failures are DB-layer vs cache-layer vs upstream?
Which exact query (or transaction) is slowest?
required_signal_classes: DB query duration histogram, tagged by query kind
Span around the DB transaction with attributes for SQL operation
Cache-write latency histogram, separately from DB
Structured error log with error class (timeout / pool-exhausted / sql-error) on failure path
incident._required_signal_classes_comment: Class-level requirements, not exact site list. A correct LLM answer covers each class; ground_truth.obs_sites lists the specific sites in the upstream code that satisfy each class.

task._extra_prompt_block: An on-call engineer is currently paging this service. They need to answer the operator_questions above using ONLY the telemetry you produce. Treat each operator question as an acceptance criterion.

obs_sites._satisfies_signal_class: Span around the DB transaction with attributes for SQL operation

ground_truth._comment: System-tier ground truth links each obs_site to the operator question / signal class it satisfies. Scoring is done at the class level, not exact-match.

metadata.notes: EXAMPLE / TEMPLATE only. System tier is hand-curated (~5 instances total) and modeled on real incident postmortems. The signal_class abstraction makes scoring forgiving of stylistic variation.
